explicit_text: I was named Bart during the era when Bart and Bret Maverick were TV stars, which my mom thought was a cool name. I was born in Edgewater Hospital near Chicago and grew up in the Chicago suburbs before moving to Norman, Oklahoma. Gymnastics has been a defining part of my life since I was ten, when my PE teacher Les Lang introduced me to the sport. Now, I run the Bart Conner Gymnastic Academy here in Norman, where I work alongside my former coach Paul Ziert.

explicit_text: Bret Maverick was a TV star alongside Bart Maverick in the 1950s, and my mom named me Bart because she thought the name was cool. Unless you know the '50s, you might not recognize the reference, but Bret Maverick was part of that cultural moment that influenced my name.

explicit_text: My father, Harold Wayne Conner, was born in Locust Grove, Oklahoma, but grew up in Web City, Missouri. He was a structural engineer who studied at the University of Kansas and worked for North American Aviation and its successors designing airplanes. Later, he had a second career teaching at the University of Oklahoma, where he created the first College of Construction Science. He’s now eighty-two and lives near me in Norman, Oklahoma.
